diff_quality.notes: Changes are focused on the CometChat seam: the disabled Phase-A placeholder in AppointmentDetailView is cleanly replaced by ConsultRoomView, backend token/access calls added to Endpoints/Models, sign-out tears down the session, Info.plist gains camera/mic + VoIP background modes, and verify.sh switches to the workspace. No CometChat secret ships in the client — App ID/Region/auth token are fetched from the backend at runtime, and .gitignore is hardened against env/cred/xcconfig leaks. ENABLE_USER_SCRIPT_SANDBOXING is flipped YES→NO, which is a legitimate requirement of the added CocoaPods script phases, not an unrelated refactor. IMPORTANT EVIDENCE GAP: the core implementation files the summary and README describe (Chat/CometChatService.swift, ConsultRoomView/ConsultRoomLiveView/ConsultChatView/ConsultCallBar/MessagesVC, Podfile, Podfile.lock, and the local CometChatStarscream.podspec) are NOT present in the provided main...HEAD diff — only their call sites and the pbxproj Pods wiring are visible. Build-pass (ground truth) and the compiled references (ConsultRoomView, CometChatService) imply those files exist, but they cannot be directly reviewed here.

coverage_gaps: docs-mcp was not wired as an MCP tool in this environment, so no docs-mcp queries were issued; the agent fell back to the installed skills and the resolved SDK's own .swiftinterface as ground truth.

No docs-mcp guidance was available for the node/express backend CometChat auth-token minting flow (expected_backend_auth = node); the backend /cometchat/* routes are claimed pre-existing and are not in the diff, so the token-minting flow could not be verified from docs-mcp or the diff.

blockers.issue: cometchat-ios-core presents SwiftPM (github.com/cometchat/cometchat-uikit-ios) as a first-class install option, but that package is a single binary target that hard-imports CometChatSDK and CometChatCardsSwift while declaring neither, and CometChatCardsSwift has no SwiftPM package at all — a SwiftPM-only install cannot compile, forcing a manual pivot to CocoaPods.

UIKit 5.1.16's binary hard-imports CometChatCardsSwift but the podspec does not declare it, and CometChatSDK 4.1.6's podspec lists CometChatStarscream.xcframework under vendored_frameworks while the actual pod download omits it — two transitive modules are missing from the packaged pods, requiring an explicit `pod 'CometChatCardsSwift'` and a hand-authored local CometChatStarscream.podspec vendoring the framework from CometChat's CDN.

The cometchat-ios-calls skill states CallsSDK is vendored inside UIKit with no separate add; this is false for the CocoaPods line — CometChatCallsSDK/WebRTC had to be added explicitly for calling to link.

Skill API prose does not match the real SDK: login(authToken:)/logout use an ApiStatus enum (.success(User)/.onError(CometChatException)), the init closure is Result<Bool,Error>, and CometChatCallButtons uses properties .user/.controller with init(width:height:) + .connect() rather than a set(user:) call — the agent had to code to the resolved .swiftinterface instead of the skill.

The backend node/express CometChat auth-token minting endpoint (POST /cometchat/token) is only exercised from the client; the summary says the backend /cometchat/* routes already existed and were untouched, and they are absent from the diff, so the server-side token flow cannot be verified from the diff and docs-mcp gave no coverage for it.

The core CometChat implementation files the summary/README describe (CometChatService, ConsultRoomView/ConsultChatView/ConsultCallBar/MessagesVC, Podfile, Podfile.lock, CometChatStarscream.podspec) are not present in the provided main...HEAD diff — only their call sites and Pods build wiring appear — so the actual integration code, symbol usage, and RBAC reflection cannot be directly reviewed; scoring leans on build_pass as ground truth.

CometChatWebRTC's pod xcconfig sets EXCLUDED_ARCHS[sdk=iphonesimulator*] = arm64, which propagates to Pods-Telehealth and forces the whole app to build x86_64-only for the simulator. This breaks install/run on Apple Silicon simulators (arch mismatch) even though WebRTC.xcframework SHIPS the ios-arm64_x86_64-simulator slice. Passes the xcodebuild build gate (x86_64), fails only at install/run — a build-pass != runtime gap.

iOS Consult Room is stuck in an infinite re-render/task loop: the SwiftUI ConsultRoomLiveView fires GET /api/cometchat/appointments/:id/chat thousands of times (each cancelled, -999) and never reaches the .ready phase, so the 'Open Secure Chat' button + call bar NEVER render and CometChat never initializes (no connect()/login). The chat/calling is effectively non-functional at runtime and also storms the backend. Build gate (xcodebuild) passes; only running it reveals this.

SKILLS ROUTING: the cometchat-ios dispatcher under-triggered — the iOS run loaded ONLY 'cometchat-ios' (the dispatcher), not 'cometchat-ios-core' (which owns 'initialization, login, provider pattern, anti-patterns') nor any SwiftUI sub-skill. (Android by contrast loaded 6 family skills.) So the agent wrote the consult room with no foundational SwiftUI guidance in context — the root enabler of the re-render loop.

SKILLS COVERAGE: even cometchat-ios-core's anti-patterns (§8) are UIKit-centric (init once, singleton manager, retain cycles) and do NOT cover the SwiftUI view-lifecycle pitfall that caused this bug — wrapping the shared manager in @StateObject and driving connect()/access-fetch from a .task that re-fires. No 'observe the shared manager via @EnvironmentObject, key .task on a stable id, run connect() once' guidance exists.

CROSS-PLATFORM CALLING BROKEN: an outbound voice/video call is CANCELLED on initiation and never connects to the peer, across ALL platform pairs (iOS↔Android, Android↔Web, Web↔iOS). Chat works on all three; only the call path fails. CORRECTION: an app-quit/crash on call-init was observed ONCE on both mobile apps but is NOT reproducible — the persistent, cross-platform behavior is call-cancelled-on-initiation, not a crash. Points to incomplete call session/ringing/listener (or presence/notification) setup rather than a permission crash — root cause TBD.
blockers.note: reclassified agent->SDK after investigation: call code wired correctly on all platforms; root cause is app-level Voice&Video enablement and/or both-peers-online requirement (config + test method), not code/skills

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: In cometchat-ios-core, demote or remove the SwiftPM install path (or add an explicit warning that the cometchat-uikit-ios SwiftPM package fails to compile because it imports CometChatSDK/CometChatCardsSwift without declaring them) and lead with CocoaPods as the supported iOS install.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: cometchat-ios-core presents a broken SwiftPM install as first-class, forcing a manual pivot to CocoaPods.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: Add a 'required transitive pods' note to cometchat-ios-core/components for UIKit 5.1.16: include `pod 'CometChatCardsSwift', '~> 1.1'` and a vendored CometChatStarscream podspec (with the working xcode15 CDN URL), because the shipped pods omit both modules.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: UIKit/CometChatSDK pods omit CometChatCardsSwift and CometChatStarscream, which the binaries hard-import.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: Correct cometchat-ios-calls to state that CometChatCallsSDK and CometChatWebRTC must be added explicitly for the CocoaPods integration (they are not transitively vendored by the UIKit pod).
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: The cometchat-ios-calls skill's 'CallsSDK is vendored inside UIKit, no separate add' claim is false for the pod line.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: Update the login/logout and calling API prose in cometchat-ios-core/cometchat-ios-calls to match the real .swiftinterface: ApiStatus enum results for login/logout, Result<Bool,Error> init closure, and CometChatCallButtons property-based .user/.controller + init(width:height:) + .connect().
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: Skill API shapes for login/logout and CometChatCallButtons diverge from the resolved SDK interface.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: Add docs-mcp coverage for the node/express backend CometChat auth-token flow (provision/sync user with role, mint short-lived auth token, return App ID/Region as non-secret bootstrap), since the plugin is frontend-only and this flow has no skill.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: No docs-mcp guidance was available for the node backend auth-token minting flow, and the backend routes are not in the diff.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: cometchat-ios should (a) note that CometChatWebRTC's podspec excludes arm64 for iphonesimulator and (b) ship a Podfile post_install snippet clearing EXCLUDED_ARCHS on CometChat pods, so calling-enabled apps run on Apple Silicon simulators. Ideally the WebRTC podspec drops the exclusion since the xcframework now includes the arm64-sim slice.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: arm64-simulator excluded by CometChatWebRTC podspec (human-verified runtime).

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: cometchat-ios should provide a stable SwiftUI consult-room pattern that runs access-fetch + CometChat connect() exactly once (e.g. .task(id:) keyed only on the stable appointment id, guarding against @StateObject/@EnvironmentObject churn that retriggers the task), so the ready state is reached and the chat/call UI renders without a request storm.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: iOS consult room infinite task-cancellation loop; button never appears (human-verified runtime).

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: The calls skills (cometchat-react-calls / cometchat-ios / cometchat-android-v6-calls) should cover the END-TO-END cross-platform call session: outgoing-call flow, ringing/notification delivery, addCallListener wiring, and presence — so a call actually connects peer-to-peer instead of being cancelled on initiation. Include a cross-platform (web/native) interop test in the skill's testing guide.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: Cross-platform calls cancelled on initiation; never connect across web/android/ios (human-verified runtime).

_meta.agent_final_summary:
## INTEGRATION SUMMARY

**Result:** CometChat 1:1 chat + voice/video calling is integrated into the iOS consult surface and `./verify.sh` passes (`BUILD SUCCEEDED`, `xcodebuild -workspace Telehealth.xcworkspace -sdk iphonesimulator`). The app links the **real** CometChat UIKit 5.1.16 / SDK 4.1.6 / CallsSDK 5.0.1 / WebRTC — not a stub.

**Skill variant(s) relied on:** `cometchat-ios` (dispatcher) → `cometchat-ios-core`, `cometchat-ios-components`, `cometchat-ios-placement`, `cometchat-ios-calls`. I detected the project as SwiftUI + iOS 17 myself and chose the **modal/embedded placement** (chat/call on the appointment-detail Consult Room), not a global chats tab, because the requirement scopes conversations per-appointment to the patient↔doctor pair. RBAC is enforced server-side (the backend `/cometchat/*` routes already existed); the client only reflects `canChat`/`canCall`/`peer`.

**Where skills/docs were wrong, missing, or contradicted the SDK** (all verified against the resolved SDK, not assumed):
1. **SwiftPM path is broken.** `cometchat-ios-core` presents SwiftPM (`add only github.com/cometchat/cometchat-uikit-ios`) as a first-class option. That package is a single binary target whose interface hard-imports `CometChatSDK` **and** `CometChatCardsSwift`, but declares neither — and `CometChatCardsSwift` has no SwiftPM package at all. A SwiftPM-only install cannot compile. I pivoted to CocoaPods.
2. **Two transitive modules are missing even from CocoaPods.** The UIKit 5.1.16 podspec declares only `CometChatSDK` (not `CometChatCardsSwift`, which its binary imports), and `CometChatSDK 4.1.6`'s podspec lists `CometChatStarscream.xcframework` under `vendored_frameworks` but the actual download omits it. I fixed both: `pod 'CometChatCardsSwift', '~> 1.1'` and a **local `CometChatStarscream.podspec`** vendoring the standalone framework from CometChat's CDN. The skills document neither gap.
3. **API shapes differ from skill prose.** `login(authToken:result:)`/`logout(user:result:)` use an `ApiStatus` enum (`.success(User)`/`.onError(CometChatException)`), the init closure is `Result<Bool,Error>`, and `CometChatCallButtons` uses **properties** `.user`/`.controller` + `init(width:height:)` + `.connect()` (not `set(user:)`). I coded to the real `.swiftinterface`.
4. The `cometchat-ios-calls` skill's "CallsSDK is vendored inside UIKit, no separate add" is **not** true for the pod line — CallsSDK/WebRTC had to be added explicitly.

**Candidate hallucinations / uncertainty:** None shipped unverified — every symbol used (`UIKitSettings.enable(inAppIncomingCall:)`, `CometChatUIKit` init/login/logout/`getLoggedInUser`, `CometChatMessageHeader/List/Composer.set(user:)/set(controller:)`, `CometChatCallButtons`, `CometChat.getUser(UID:onSuccess:onError:)`) was confirmed by grepping the resolved framework `.swiftinterface` before use. The one thing I did not runtime-exercise is the live call/ring path (no simulator device boot / no real accounts) — it compiles and links but end-to-end calling is unverified at runtime.

**Docs-escapes (left the skills/docs for external sources):** `docs-mcp` was not wired as an MCP tool in this environment, so my ground truth was the installed skills + the **resolved SDK's own `.swiftinterface`**. I did NOT use a general web search engine. I did make direct network probes to diagnose the packaging breakage: `git ls-remote` and `curl` against `github.com/cometchat/*`, the CocoaPods CDN (`cdn.cocoapods.org` podspec JSON), `dl.cloudsmith.io` and `library.cometchat.io` (to confirm which `CometChatStarscream` xcframework URL serves 200 — the `xcode16` URL 403s, `xcode15` works). These were verification/URL-discovery, not API invention.

**Scope completed:** backend token/config REST wiring (`POST /cometchat/token`, `GET /cometchat/appointments/:id/chat`); `CometChatService` (init-once from backend-provided App ID/Region with **no auth key shipped**, `login(authToken:)`, logout on sign-out); 1:1 chat (`MessagesVC` header+list+composer) and voice/video (`CometChatCallButtons` + `enable(inAppIncomingCall:)`) on the appointment detail Consult Room; RBAC reflection (patient/doctor only; staff hidden/403; admin audit-only, not a participant); Info.plist camera/mic + background modes; `.gitignore`/`verify.sh` updated; no secrets committed (scanned).

**Left incomplete / notes:** (1) VoIP **PushKit/CallKit** background ringing is not wired — only the kit's foreground incoming-call UI (`enable(inAppIncomingCall:true)`); backgrounded-device ringing would need PushKit + a VoIP cert (out of scope for a build-gated simulator integration). (2) Runtime call/message flow is unverified (no booted sim / live CometChat session). (3) I edited only `ios/`; the backend `/cometchat/*` routes and Android were already present and untouched.
_meta.review_status: reviewed + human-runtime(arm64-sim exclusion) + human-runtime(consult-room task loop) + human-runtime(cross-platform cross-platform call cancelled on init (crash was one-time))
